claims.claim: Switzerland's installed data-center base was 850.60 MW in 2025, projected to grow to 950.91 MW by 2031 at 1.99% CAGR.
claims.sourceTitle: Switzerland Data Center Market Size & Growth to 2031
claims.excerpt: Base Year Market Size (2025) | 850.60 megawatt ... Market Volume (2031) | 950.91 megawatt | Growth Rate (2026 - 2031) | 1.99% CAGR

claims.claim: Switzerland's commercial (non-self-operated) data-center capacity is 340 MW across 121 sites, totaling ~428,000 m² built area including 229,000 m² of IT white space.
claims.sourceTitle: CBRE Suisse publie les dernières tendances et chiffres du marché des datacenters en Suisse
claims.excerpt: La capacité actuellement disponible sur le marché suisse des datacenters, hors infrastructures exploitées pour compte propre, s'élève à 340 MW et est répartie sur 121 sites. Ceux-ci occupent une surface totale bâtie d'environ 428'000 m², dont 229'000 m² de surfaces utiles IT

claims.claim: Zurich region concentrates 61% (209 MW) of Switzerland's total commercial data-center capacity.
claims.sourceTitle: CBRE Suisse publie les dernières tendances et chiffres du marché des datacenters en Suisse
claims.excerpt: Environ 61% de la capacité totale suisse, soit 209 MW, se concentre actuellement dans la région de Zurich.

claims.claim: The Lake Geneva region (Geneva and Vaud cantons) totals 60 MW, or 18% of Switzerland's national data-center capacity.
claims.sourceTitle: CBRE Suisse publie les dernières tendances et chiffres du marché des datacenters en Suisse
claims.excerpt: La région lémanique (cantons de Genève et de Vaud) totalise quant à elle 60 MW, soit 18% de la capacité du pays.

claims.claim: GTR announced a 24MW data-center facility (CH One) in Zurich, spanning 11,315 sqm across three data halls, first phase live 2028.
claims.sourceTitle: GTR plans 24MW data center in Zurich, Switzerland
claims.excerpt: The company this week announced CH One, a 24MW facility located in Zurich. The three-story facility will span 11,315 sqm (121,793 sq ft) across three data halls. The first phase is expected to go live in 2028.

claims.claim: Switzerland's data center market is valued at USD 2.9 billion (base year 2024, five-year historical analysis).
claims.sourceTitle: Switzerland Data Center Market | 2019 – 2030 - Ken Research
claims.excerpt: The Switzerland Data Center Market is valued at USD 2.9 billion, based on a five-year historical analysis.

claims.claim: Switzerland's data center market was valued at USD 960 million in 2025, expected to reach USD 2.47 billion by 2031 at 17.09% CAGR.
claims.sourceTitle: Switzerland Data Center Market Size, Investment, Growth ... - Arizton
claims.excerpt: THE SWITZERLAND DATA CENTER MARKET SIZE WAS VALUED AT USD 960 MILLION IN 2025 AND IS EXPECTED TO REACH USD 2.47 BILLION BY 2031, GROWING AT A CAGR OF 17.09% DURING THE FORECAST PERIOD.

claims.claim: Data centres could consume up to 15% of Swiss electricity by 2030.
claims.sourceTitle: Can Switzerland's power grid keep up with its data centres? - SWI swissinfo.ch
claims.excerpt: Hungry servers: data centres could consume up to 15% of Swiss electricity by 2030.

claims.claim: Switzerland has among the highest number of data centres per capita in the world, and among the highest data-center electricity share in Europe.
claims.sourceTitle: Can Switzerland's power grid keep up with its data centres? - SWI swissinfo.ch
claims.excerpt: In Switzerland, the number of data centres per capita is among the highest in the world, while the share of electricity they consume is among the highest in Europe.

claims.sourceTitle: Austria Data Center Market Size & Share Outlook to 2031
claims.excerpt: Base Year Market Size (2025) | 207 megawatt ... Market Volume (2031) | 342.54 megawatt | Growth Rate (2026 - 2031) | 8.94% CAGR

claims.claim: Austria's data center market was valued at USD 1.18 billion in 2025, expected to reach USD 1.86 billion by 2031 at 7.91% CAGR; power capacity projected to hit 33 MW by 2031.
claims.sourceTitle: Austria Data Center Market Size & Investment Growth Forecast 2031
claims.excerpt: Market Area to Reach 129 Thousand Sq. Ft., Power Capacity to Hit 33 MW, and Colocation Revenue to Surpass USD 350 Million by 2031

claims.claim: The cost of building a data center in Austria is typically estimated at around $11-$12 million per megawatt of IT load.
claims.sourceTitle: Austria Data Center Market - Investment Analysis & Growth Opportunities 2026-2031
claims.excerpt: The cost of building a data center in Austria is typically estimated at around $11-$12 million per megawatt (MW) of IT load, positioning it in the mid-to-upper range within Europe.

claims.claim: Vienna has 12 data-center facilities totaling 750,245 sqft and 78 megawatts.
claims.excerpt: Vienna has 12 facilities with 750,245 sqft and 78 megawatts.

claims.claim: Vienna's data-center power capacity grew 87% since 2022.
claims.excerpt: Vienna Power Growth Over Time +87% since 2022

claims.claim: Austria operates 28 registered colocation facilities per PeeringDB data (March 2026), with 22 concentrated in Vienna; Vienna accounts for 79% of national colocation capacity.
claims.sourceTitle: Colocation Austria: VIX, Vienna Hub | RebootMonkey
claims.excerpt: Austria operates 28 registered colocation facilities according to PeeringDB data (March 2026), with 22 concentrated in Vienna and the remainder distributed across Graz, Linz, and Salzburg. Vienna accounts for 79% of national colocation capacity

claims.claim: Mordor Intelligence projects Austria's data center market to reach USD 1.1 billion by 2030, growing at 15.18% CAGR from a USD 474 million 2024 base; Vienna holds 74.2% of national market share; colocation represents 67.6% of Austrian data center revenue.
claims.sourceTitle: Colocation Austria: VIX, Vienna Hub | RebootMonkey
claims.excerpt: Mordor Intelligence projects Austria's data center market to reach USD 1.1 billion by 2030, growing at 15.18% CAGR from a USD 474 million 2024 base. Vienna holds 74.2% of that national market share, and colocation represents 67.6% of Austrian data center revenue.

claims.claim: Vienna hosts over 80% of Austria's total IT capacity.
claims.sourceTitle: Vienna's Data Centre Market: Navigating Growth, Challenges, and the Path Ahead | DC Byte
claims.excerpt: Vienna has long been the beating heart of the Austrian data centre market, hosting over 80% of the country's IT capacity.

claims.claim: Vienna's data center market slowdown is driven by power constraints, escalating construction costs, and supply chain delays, causing it to be overshadowed by faster-growing European hubs.
claims.sourceTitle: Vienna's Data Centre Market: Navigating Growth, Challenges, and the Path Ahead | DC Byte
claims.excerpt: Power constraints, escalating construction costs, and supply chain delays have significantly slowed the delivery of new capacity. As a result, Vienna is now being overshadowed by other European hubs that are growing at a much faster pace.

tensions.text: Baxtel independently measures Vienna alone at 78 MW of colocation capacity, and multiple sources (RebootMonkey 79%, DC Byte >80%) place Vienna's share of national colocation capacity at ~80%. This implies Austria's national colocation total is roughly 97-100 MW, well above the ~68 MW colo figure implied in geo.AT's Statista-based apportionment (68 MW ÷ 3,451 MW = 0.02), suggesting the node's colo baseline may understate Austrian colocation capacity.

Arizton projects Austria's power capacity to hit only 33 MW by 2031, while Mordor Intelligence (cited in geo.AT's own cross-check) projects 342.54 MW installed base by 2031 for the same country and year — a roughly 10x discrepancy in the same nominal metric (MW capacity), undermining confidence in either figure as a stable denominator/numerator for the ratio.

Switzerland's data-center market valuation diverges sharply between sources: Ken Research cites USD 2.9 billion vs Arizton's USD 960 million for 2025 — a ~3x gap reflecting scope/definition inconsistency, echoing geo.CH's own provenance note that 'scope choice is decisive' for Swiss market sizing.

CBRE Suisse's regional breakdown (Zurich 209 MW = 61%, Lake Geneva 60 MW = 18%) sums to 269 MW of the 340 MW commercial total (79%), leaving only 71 MW (21%) for all other Swiss regions combined — a concentration pattern not reflected in the node's flat 0.08 ratio applied uniformly across CE-7 comparison.

absences: No source in this batch independently confirms the Statista CE-7 colocation figures (CH 274 MW, AT 68 MW, CE-7 total 3,451 MW) that anchor both nodes' primary derivation.

No breakdown was found of Austria's self-operated (non-colocation) capacity comparable to CBRE's Swiss commercial-vs-self-operated split, leaving the AT node's installed-base vs colo-scope divergence unquantified on the Austrian side.
No current MW figure for Basel or other secondary Swiss hotspots beyond Zurich and Lake Geneva was found, despite Ken Research naming Basel as a dominant hub.
No data center count or capacity figures for Graz, Linz, or Salzburg were quantified, despite RebootMonkey naming them as secondary Austrian markets.

No search result reconciled the conflicting Austria CAGR figures (8.94% MW CAGR per Mordor's own MW report vs 15.18% revenue CAGR attributed to Mordor via RebootMonkey vs 7.91% value CAGR per Arizton).
